title: cachrys tuberculata
intro: Cachrys tuberculata, also known as Desert Celery or Chervil, is a species of flowering plant in the carrot family Apiaceae. It is native to the deserts of Iran and the Middle East. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, and is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever and improving digestion. Cachrys tuberculata can be prepared as a herbal tea, or used as a fresh herb in various dishes, and its roots and seeds can also be used to make infusions and tinctures.
